questions: MeteoriteEntrance is a complex multi-phase effect (approach → impact → shake → flash). What should the exit version look like? Options: (a) full reversal (shake → fly away), (b) simple fade/zoom-out fallback for exit mode, (c) skip meteorite for exit (entrance-only). Recommendation: (a) reversed phases.

For continuous looping, should one ping-pong cycle span the entire clip duration, or should there be a fixed cycle period (e.g., 3 seconds)? One cycle per clip means a 2-second clip loops fast and a 30-second clip loops very slowly. A fixed period is more consistent but adds a parameter.

For the legacy exit mapping: should old exit type names be silently mapped at render time (transparent to user), or should we run a one-time config migration that rewrites saved timelines? Render-time mapping is simpler but means the raw config still has old names.

Custom effects currently have a category ('entrance'|'exit'|'continuous'). After unification, should the EffectCreatorPanel still let users pick entrance vs exit, or collapse to 'clip-effect' vs 'continuous'?

The brief says loop default=true. This means existing one-shot continuous effects (KenBurns, SlowZoom, Drift) on existing timelines will start looping. Is this the desired behavior, or should existing configs default to loop=false for backwards compat?

success_criteria.criterion: All 11 entrance effects render correctly in both entrance and exit modes
Saved timeline configs with old exit type names (slide-down, zoom-out, fade-out, flip, shrink, dissolve) render correctly without config migration
Custom effects (compiled via Sucrase, registered in DynamicEffectRegistry) continue to work as their saved category
ClipPanel UI shows unified effect catalog in both entrance and exit dropdowns
Continuous effects KenBurns, SlowZoom, Drift support loop toggle
Loop toggle visible in ClipPanel continuous section
exits.tsx is deleted; no duplicate effect implementations remain
No new files beyond renaming entrances.tsx → clip-effects.tsx
MeteoriteEntrance works reasonably in exit mode (reversed or graceful fallback)
effectPromptTemplate updated to explain bidirectional effects and loop param to LLM
Ping-pong looping looks smooth with no visible jump at cycle boundaries
Manual smoke test: create a clip, apply entrance effect, apply same effect as exit, verify both animate correctly
Manual smoke test: toggle loop on/off for KenBurns continuous effect, verify behavior change

assumptions: The data model (ClipEntrance, ClipExit as separate fields on TimelineClip) stays unchanged — unification is at the component/catalog level only, not the serialization format.
Legacy exit type names are resolved at render time via a mapping table, not via a config migration. This avoids touching saved data.
Custom effects that don't know about the `mode` prop will play forward in both entrance and exit slots. This is acceptable; new custom effects can opt into bidirectionality.
For continuous looping, one ping-pong cycle spans the full clip duration. This can be refined later with a cycle duration parameter.
The `loop` field defaults to `true` when absent, meaning existing one-shot continuous effects will start looping. This matches the brief's intent that 'continuous to mean continuous by default.'
Spring-based effects (SlideUpEntrance) will use linear interpolation in exit mode rather than attempting to reverse the spring physics.
The EffectCreatorPanel category selector keeps 'entrance'|'exit'|'continuous' in the DB for backwards compat but the UI groups entrance+exit as 'Clip Effect'.
